question_text: Explain why X-rays are dangerous to human tissue but radio waves are not, even though both are electromagnetic waves.
correct_answer: X-rays have much higher frequency/shorter wavelength and carry more energy, allowing them to ionize atoms in cells and damage DNA. Radio waves have lower energy and cannot ionize biological tissue.
explanation: Higher frequency electromagnetic waves carry more energy per photon. X-rays have enough energy to knock electrons off atoms (ionization), which can damage living cells and cause cancer. Radio waves lack this energy and pass through tissue safely.
